input.title: Self-reported CJC-1295 use and IGF-1 levels among recreational bodybuilders: a cross-sectional survey
input.abstract: A cross-sectional survey and blood sampling study was conducted at two fitness expositions. Of 310 male recreational bodybuilders surveyed (mean age 29 ± 6 years), 78 (25%) reported current or recent CJC-1295 use (within 30 days). Current users had significantly higher serum IGF-1 levels (318 ± 74 ng/mL) compared with non-users (214 ± 52 ng/mL; p<0.001, adjusted for age, BMI, and steroid co-use). Self-reported dose ranged from 0.5 to 2 mg subcutaneous twice weekly. This cross-sectional observation is limited by self-report bias and cannot establish causality, but suggests meaningful IGF-1 elevation associated with CJC-1295 use in this population.
